leadText: This is a list of all items that appear in the internal data of Pokémon games, but are not obtainable as inventory items by any legitimate means in that game or set of games. This does not include items which are carried over between successive game releases within a particular generation, such as items in Pokémon Ruby and Sapphire that remain in the internal data of Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen. Instead, it includes items that are not available in non-successive game releases or in any game within a particular generation, items which availability differs between regional releases of one or more games within a particular generation, and items that are used and programmed in as inventory items but that are otherwise unobtainable (only accessible) during regular gameplay.

sections.title: In the core series games

KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows surfing over water without the need for Pokémon and/or Badges . Locations R B Y None (original intended location unknown; it appears to be just a debugging item) Secondary effect: Allows surfing on Route 17 's Cycling Road. This item can even be used in a battle , as long as it has started while the player is facing a tile they can surf on. The player will be treated as if they were surfing, changing the background music to The Sea . Because it comes from a different soundbank than sound effects used in battle, attempting to play them will refer to that soundbank, causing the wrong sounds to be played. When the battle ends, the player will move one tile forward. This can be exploited to pass through barriers. Note: Index number 0x07. Often nicknamed "Surfboard" by players. Safari Ball サファリボール Safari Ball KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows the player to catch wild Pokémon in the Safari Zone . Catch rate: 1.5× Locations R B Y Safari Zone ; Cheating (outside of the Safari Zone) Description Stad A Ball used in the Safari Zone for catching wild Pok é mon . Note: Index number 0x08 in Generation I. Unlike in later generations, it is a Key Item. Pokédex ポケモンずかん Illustrated Pokémon encyclopedia KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Opens the Pokédex both during and outside of battle. Locations R B Y None (intended to be given to the player by Professor Oak at his lab , probably after delivering him his parcel ) Secondary effect: Causes the VRAM to load the overworld tileset and turns the HP bar to letters when used in battle. Note: Index number 0x09. Replaced by the Pokédex function in the menu . BoulderBadge グレーバッジ Grey Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Throws Bait , like in the Safari Zone . Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Brock at Pewter Gym ) Secondary effect: When used in the overworld (except dungeons and caves ), it changes the background music to a single channel of Guide ; if used in a dungeon or cave, it changes the background music to a single channel of Title Screen . This is due to the function that plays the "throw Bait or Rock" sound effect attempting to load it but referring to the wrong soundbank. Note: Index number 0x15. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. CascadeBadge ブルーバッジ Blue Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Throws a Rock, like in the Safari Zone . Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Misty at Cerulean Gym ) Secondary effect: When used in the overworld (except dungeons and caves ), it changes the background music to a single channel of Guide ; if used in a dungeon or cave, it changes the background music to a single channel of Title Screen . This is due to the function that plays the "throw Bait or Rock" sound effect attempting to load it but referring to the wrong soundbank. Note: Index number 0x16. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. ThunderBadge オレンジバッジ Orange Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Lt. Surge at Vermilion Gym ) Note: Index number 0x17. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. RainbowBadge レインボーバッジ Rainbow Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Erika at Celadon Gym ) Note: Index number 0x18. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. SoulBadge ピンクバッジ Pink Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Koga at Fuchsia Gym ) Note: Index number 0x19. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. MarshBadge ゴールドバッジ Gold Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Sabrina at Saffron Gym ) Note: Index number 0x1A. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. VolcanoBadge クリムゾンバッジ Crimson Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Blaine at Cinnabar Gym ) Note: Index number 0x1B. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. EarthBadge グリーンバッジ Green Badge KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (intended to be reward for defeating Giovanni as a Gym Leader at Viridian Gym ) Note: Index number 0x1C. Along with the other Badge Key Items, it is internally used in the list of Badges presented by the Gym Badge man in Cerulean City . Treated like a regular item in Stadium. ????? ????? ????? KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None Note: Index number 0x2C. PP Up ポイントアップ Point Up ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 9800 Sell for: $ 4900 Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None (probably intended to be the actual PP Up , suggesting it was to be found at the same multiple locations) Note: Index number 0x32; the actual PP Up has index number 0x4F. While the actual PP Up can be sold and/or bought for $ 0, this one can be sold for $ 4900 and bought for $ 9800. Coin コイン Coin ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 10 Sell for: $ 5 Effect: No effect. Locations R B Y None Note: Index number 0x3B. It can be bought for half the price of actual Coin Case-stored Coins in the Game Corner , which cost $ 20 per Coin. It cannot be used in the slot machines, exchanged for prizes, or put into a Coin Case. It also appears listed in the Trainer's Guide (manual) of Red and Blue as a "miscellaneous item", being the only item that fits into this category. TM51 わざマシン 51 Move Machine 51 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 3000 Sell for: $ 1500 Effect: Teaches a Pokémon Cut . Locations R B Y None (original intended location unknown) Note: Index number 0xFB. TM52 わざマシン 52 Move Machine 52 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 14000 Sell for: $ 7000 Effect: Teaches a Pokémon Fly . Locations R B Y None (original intended location unknown) Note: Index number 0xFC. TM53 わざマシン 53 Move Machine 53 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 0 Cannot be sold * Effect: Teaches a Pokémon Surf . Locations R B Y None (original intended location unknown) Note: Index number 0xFD. TM54 わざマシン 54 Move Machine 54 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 8000 Sell for: $ 4000 Effect: Teaches a Pokémon Strength . Locations R B Y None (original intended location unknown) Note: Index number 0xFE. TM55 わざマシン 55 Move Machine 55 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN I Price: $ 4000 Sell for: $ 2000 Effect: Teaches a Pokémon Flash . Locations R B Y None (original intended location unknown) Secondary effect: Appears as Cancel (Japanese: やめる Cancel ) and hides items below it. Note: Index number 0xFF. Generation II [ edit | edit source ] ? ？ ? ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (it is a placeholder for item data and may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Secondary effect: If held by a Pokémon , the game behaves as if the Pokémon is not holding an item . Triggers a crash in all localizations of Crystal due to an oversight. [ 1 ] Description G S ? C Japanese: ヘ・を こうげきこうげきご……。 Note: Index number 0x00. The Japanese Crystal description is generated arbitrarily due to an oversight and is meaningless. Teru-sama カビチュウ Kabichū ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (it is a placeholder for item data and may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Secondary effect: Variations with index numbers 0x46 , 0x73 , 0x74 , and 0x81 become the Clear Bell , GS Ball , Blue Card , and Egg Ticket in Crystal , respectively. Variations with index numbers 0x06 and 0x38 are the Town Map (broken) and Poké Flute from the Generation I games, respectively. Variation with index number 0xBE triggers an invalid pointer when its usage is forced. Description G S C ? Note: There are 29 variations of this item in Gold and Silver and 25 in Crystal. Park Ball パークボール Park Ball POKÉ BALLS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN II Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows the player to catch wild Pokémon at the Bug-Catching Contest in the National Park . Catch rate: 1.5× Locations G S C Only accessible during the Bug-Catching Contest at the National Park in regular gameplay; can be obtained as an item in the player 's Bag via cheating or the Celebi Egg glitch Secondary effect: Causes a temporary graphical glitch when used from the Ball pocket of the Bag . Description G S C The Bug-Catching Contest Ball . Note: Index number 0xB1 in Generation II. It should not be confused with the Park Ball (Japanese: パークボール Park Ball ) used at the Pal Park in the Generation IV games. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, it was renamed to Sport Ball (Japanese: コンペボール Compé Ball ) precisely to avoid this confusion. Bluesky Mail あおぞらメール Blue Sky Mail ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 25 Effect: Causes the secondary effect of Thief to fail when used on holder. Pokémon holding this cannot be placed in the PC . Locations C PokéCom Center (hard "Pokémon Character Maze" prize; ×3) * [ 2 ] Description G S C Sky-print Mail . (HOLD) Mirage Mail まぼろしメール Illusory Mail ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 25 Effect: Causes the secondary effect of Thief to fail when used on holder. Pokémon holding this cannot be placed in the PC . Locations C PokéCom Center * Description G S C Mew -print Mail . (HOLD) TM04 わざマシン 04 Move Machine 04 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Secondary effect: When held by a Pokémon, its name is displayed as Berry (Japanese: きのみ Berry ). Description G S C Attacks 5 turns with rising power. Note: Index number 0xC3 and is stored in the Items pocket of the player's Bag; the real TM04 has index number 0xC2 and is stored in the TMs and HMs pocket of the player's Bag. TM28 わざマシン 28 Move Machine 28 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Secondary effect: When held by a Pokémon, its name is displayed as Berry (Japanese: きのみ Berry ). Description G S C 1st turn: Burrow 2nd turn: Attack Note: Index number 0xDC and is stored in the Items pocket of the player's Bag; the real TM28 has index number 0xDB and is stored in the TMs and HMs pocket of the player's Bag. HM08 ひでんマシン 08 Secret Machine 08 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Description G S ? C An attack that may inflict a burn. Note: Index number 0xFA. Used internally on the list of moves shown by the Move Tutor in Crystal, hence the move description of Flamethrower. HM09 ひでんマシン 09 Secret Machine 09 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Description G S ? C An attack that may cause paralysis. Note: Index number 0xFB. Used internally on the list of moves shown by the Move Tutor in Crystal, hence the move description of Thunderbolt. HM10 ひでんマシン 10 Secret Machine 10 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via cheating ) Description G S ? C An attack that may freeze the foe. Note: Index number 0xFC. Used internally on the list of moves shown by the Move Tutor in Crystal, hence the move description of Ice Beam. HM11 ひでんマシン 11 Secret Machine 11 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Description G S C ? Note: Index number 0xFD. HM12 ひでんマシン 12 Secret Machine 12 ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 19660 Effect: No effect. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Description G S C Attacks 5 turns with rising power. Note: Index number 0xFE. Has Rollout's move description. Cancel やめる Cancel UNKNOWN pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Does the same thing as the Cancel option. Locations G S C None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via Celebi Egg glitch or cheating ) Secondary effect: Hides items below it. Description G S C (This item has no description.) Note: Index number 0xFF. It is similar to TM55 (0xFF) in the Generation I games. It does not trigger the USE/GIVE/TOSS/SEL/CANCEL menu, it cannot be held by a Pokémon, and it cannot be purchased or sold. Pokémon Crystal [ edit | edit source ] GS Ball ジーエスボール GS Ball KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Summons Celebi in Ilex Forest . Locations C Pokémon Communication Center (Japanese version) or Goldenrod Pokémon Center (international releases) (requires event to be triggered first); Celebi Egg glitch or Cheating (all releases) Description C Japanese: ふしぎな ふんいきの ボール English: The mysterious Ball . Note: Index number 0x73. Egg Ticket タマゴけん Egg Ticket KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN II Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Exchange at the Pokémon Communication Center for an Odd Egg in the Japanese version of Crystal . This is no longer possible due to the shutdown of the Mobile System GB . Locations C Pokémon Day Care on Route 34 (Japanese version); Celebi Egg glitch or Cheating (all releases) Description C Japanese: コガネシティの トレードコーナー で つかえる らしい English: May use at Goldenrod trade corner . Note: Index number 0x81. Generation III [ edit | edit source ] Safari Ball サファリボール Safari Ball POKÉ BALLS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows the player to catch wild Pokémon in the Safari Zone . Catch rate: 1.5× Locations R S E Safari Zone ; Cheating (outside of the Safari Zone) FR LG Safari Zone ; Cheating (outside of the Safari Zone) Description R S E A special ball that is used only in the Safari Zone . FR LG A special ball that is used only in the Safari Zone . It is finished with a camouflage pattern. Note: Index number 0x0005 in all Generation III, IV, V, and VI games. Berry Juice きのみジュース Berry Juice MEDICINE pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN II Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 50 Effect: Restores 20 HP . Can be activated as a held item . Locations R S E None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via cheating , or by trading from FireRed and LeafGreen ) FR LG None (intended to be found as a held item by wild Shuckle at Altering Cave after unlocking the data from a specific Mystery Gift event; may be obtained via cheating ) Description R S E A 100% pure juice that restores HP by 20 points. FR LG A 100% pure juice. It restores the HP of one Pok é mon by 20 points. Note: Index number 0x002C in all Generation III games. It is programmed to be held by wild Shuckle in FireRed and LeafGreen; however, the only way to encounter one in these games is through Altering Cave 's unused data. Since it's impossible to trade with Generations II or IV, this item remains unavailable outside of cheating in the Generation III games. Old Sea Map ふるびたかいず Old Sea Chart KEY ITEMS pocket ONLY IN GEN III Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows access to Faraway Island . Locations E Nintendo event (Japanese version); Cheating (international releases) Description E A faded sea chart that shows the way to a certain island. Note: Index number 0x0178. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Mosaic Mail モザイクメール Mosaic Mail MAIL pocket ONLY IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 25 Effect: Causes the secondary effect of Thief and Covet to fail when used on holder. Causes Trick and Switcheroo to fail when used by or on the holder. Causes Fling to fail when used by holder. Pokémon holding this cannot be placed in the PC . Locations D P Pt None (original intended location unknown, may be obtained via cheating ) HG SS None (original intended location unknown, may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS Stationery featuring a print of a vivid rainbow pattern. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery. Note: Index number 0x0093 in all Generation IV games. Generation IV onwards [ edit | edit source ] Safari Ball サファリボール Safari Ball POKÉ BALLS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN I Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows the player to catch wild Pokémon in the Safari Zone . Catch rate: 1.5× Locations D P Pt Great Marsh ; Cheating (outside of the Great Marsh) HG SS Safari Zone ; Cheating (outside of the Safari Zone) B W None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation VI and may be obtained via cheating ) Sw Sh Cram-o-matic (any 4 Apricorns , 0.1% probability) Description D P Pt HG SS A special Poké Ball that is used only in the Great Marsh. It is decorated in a camouflage pattern. B W B2 W2 A special Poké Ball that is used only in the Great Marsh. It is decorated in a camouflage pattern. X Y OR AS A special Poké Ball that is used only in the Great Marsh. It is recognizable by the camouflage pattern decorating it. S M A special Poké Ball that is used only in the Great Marsh. It is recognizable by the camouflage pattern decorating it. Sw Sh A special Poké Ball that was used in the Safari Zone in the Kanto region and in the Great Marsh in the Sinnoh region. Note: Index number 0x0005 in all Generation III, IV, V, VI games. The description in HeartGold and SoulSilver was not altered from Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum since it still refers to the Great Marsh; it remains unaltered in the Generation V, VI, and VII games. Cherish Ball プレジャスボール Precious Ball POKÉ BALLS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Sell for: $ 500 Effect: Contains event Pokémon . Catch rate: 1× Locations D P Pt None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) HG SS None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) B W None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (it is the Poké Ball used for storing event Pokémon ; may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS A quite rare Poké Ball that has been specially crafted to commemorate an occasion of some sort. B W B2 W2 A quite rare Poké Ball that has been specially crafted to commemorate an occasion of some sort. X Y OR AS A quite rare Poké Ball that has been crafted in order to commemorate a special occasion of some sort. S M A quite rare Poké Ball that has been crafted in order to commemorate a special occasion of some sort. Note: Index number 0x0010 in all Generation IV, V, VI, and VII games. Loot Sack たからぶくろ Treasure Sack KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Stores items obtained in the Underground . D P Pt Locations D P Pt None (original intended location unknown; however, given its description, it was likely to be found at Oreburgh Mine and may be obtained via cheating ) HG SS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum and may be obtained via cheating ) B W None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation VI and may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry loot obtained in the coal mine. B W B2 W2 A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry loot obtained in the coal mine. X Y OR AS A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry any treasures or loot obtained in the coal mine. S M A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry any treasures or loot obtained in the coal mine. Note: Index number 0x01AD in all Generation IV, V, VI, and VII games. Its purpose is fulfilled by the Underground menu. Rule Book ルールブック Rule Book KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Allows the player to select special rules for battles . Locations D P Pt None (intended to be obtained at the basement of any Pokémon Center the first time it is entered; may be obtained via cheating ) HG SS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum and may be obtained via cheating ) B W None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation VI and may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS It lists the rules for holding battles. For linked battles, you may choose which set of rules you wish to use. B W B2 W2 It lists the rules for holding battles. For linked battles, you may choose which set of rules you wish to use. X Y OR AS It lists the rules for holding battles. For Link Battles, you may choose which set of rules you wish to use. S M It lists the rules for holding battles. For Link Battles, you may choose which set of rules you wish to use. Note: Index number 0x01AE in all Generation IV, V, VI, and VII games. Seal Bag シールぶくろ Seal Sack KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Holds up to 10 seals . D P Pt Locations D P Pt None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via the Morphing glitch or cheating ) HG SS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum and may be obtained via the Morphing glitch or cheating ) B W None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation VI and may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS A tiny bag that can hold ten Seals for decorating Poké Balls. B W A tiny bag that can hold ten Seals for decorating Poké Balls. B2 W2 A tiny bag that can hold 10 Seals for decorating Poké Balls. X Y OR AS A tiny bag that can hold 10 Seals for decorating Poké Balls. S M A tiny bag that can hold 10 Seals for decorating Poké Balls. Note: Index number 0x01B4 in all Generation IV, V, VI, and VII games. Apparently replaced by the Seal Case . Red Chain あかいくさり Red Chain KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: No effect. Locations D P Pt None (original intended location unknown; may be obtained via cheating ) HG SS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum and may be obtained via cheating ) B W None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation VI and may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS A mythical chain that is said to link the legendary Pokémon that created the Sinnoh region. Oak's Letter オーキドのてがみ Ōkido 's Letter KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: A letter from Professor Oak to be taken to Route 224 ; allows access to battle Shaymin . D P Pt Locations D P Pt None (intended to be obtained via Mystery Gift event; may be obtained via cheating ) D P Available only by Mystery Gift event Pt HG SS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum and may be obtained via cheating ) B W None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) B2 W2 None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation IV and may be obtained via cheating ) X Y None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) OR AS None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation V and may be obtained via cheating ) S M None (original intended location unknown; it is likely just a leftover from Generation VI and may be obtained via cheating ) Description D P Pt HG SS A letter from Prof. Oak. Note: Index number 0x01C4 in all Generation IV, V, VI, and VII games. Azure Flute てんかいのふえ Azure Flute KEY ITEMS pocket INTRODUCED IN GEN IV Cannot be bought Cannot be sold Effect: Plays a melody that's beyond this world; opens the stairway to an Arceus in the Hall of Origin .
